findings.problem: The chapter's final movement lands Owen in custodianship, but the decisive turn is assigned by apparatus more than by his last human choice: the undercroft absorbs the manuscript for him, Tallow lays out the regime, and Owen's final labor follows an externally imposed arrangement. That softens the last inch of prize-level ferocity after the Great Hall scenes have done the harder moral work.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the ending pivot beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:265 and the maintenance assignment beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:295 so Owen performs one irreversible act that commits him to this life rather than merely receiving it. Keep the absorbed-manuscript outcome if desired, but add a choice at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:275 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:323 where he knowingly gives up a human alternative, destroys or surrenders something of his own, or initiates the maintenance protocol before Tallow can define him by it.
findings.acceptance_test: By the end of the revised passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:323, a reader can point to one explicit action or decision Owen takes that irrevocably binds him to custodianship; if the stone and Tallow still do the assigning while Owen only complies, this remains a fail.

findings.problem: The node's behavior reads as too conveniently exact at the chapter's biggest logistical turn. Owen needs a hiding solution, and the undercroft immediately absorbs the Leaves; the one page that stays legible is the maintenance material the ending needs; the text does not yet make fully clear why this happens now, why this subset remains readable, or what concrete new cost or uncertainty comes with the arrangement.
findings.rewrite_direction: Strengthen the causal chain across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:213, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:221, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:267, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:289. Give Owen or Tallow a concrete explanation grounded in battle damage, prior attunement, node load, manuscript composition, or house logic for why the Leaves can be taken now; specify why the maintenance hand rather than lore remains accessible; and add an immediate risk, limit, or inconvenience that prevents the beat from feeling like magical document storage on exactly the needed terms.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:221, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:267, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:289, a first-time reader can answer all three questions from the page itself: why the absorption happens now, why the maintenance matter remains readable, and what new cost or restriction this creates. If any of those answers still depend on inference alone, this remains a fail.

findings.problem: The chapter repeats a clipped corrective-fragment construction in narration at key beats: "Not agreement. Classification.", "Not with light. With recognition.", and "Not lore. Upkeep." This is both a visible prose tic and a direct violation of the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration. Once it recurs across multiple climax lines, it stops reading as forceful compression and starts reading as authored shorthand.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the clipped narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:159, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:267, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:289 into grammatically complete sentences that keep the pressure but do not rely on subjectless corrections. Vary the surrounding sentence shapes as well so the chapter is not leaning on the same negation-then-correction rhythm for emphasis.
findings.acceptance_test: The revised lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:159, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:267, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:289 read as complete sentences rather than clipped noun fragments, and a reread of the chapter finds this corrective fragment pattern no more than twice total. If those three beats still use subject-dropped corrections, this remains a fail.

summary: Strong physical aftermath writing, unsanitized consequence, and sharply separated Martha/Prue/Tallow voices make this a serious chapter, but it does not clear pass-level yet. The ending's custodial turn is too externally assigned, the manuscript absorption is under-motivated at the exact point it solves a plot problem, and the repeated clipped narration fragments are a clear prose failure under the constitution.
